iterations.title: Map state/CLI for numbering + seed, and design both
Resolve the forks and plan the id/numbering/seed slices
E1: id-keyed TrackingStore + NNNN-slug folders + migration
E2: -w by id or slug + id-ordered listings + id in output
E3: lume seed verb (id 0 + seed attribute + discovery iteration)

plan.sketch: E1: reshape TrackingStore to id-keyed + store-minted ids. create_workstream(slug, seed=False)->id; has/read/write(id,...); list()->[id]; id is an opaque str. FilesystemStore mints zero-padded sequential ids + names folders NNNN-slug (seed=0000 via seed flag); SQLite/in-memory mint their own id sequences. workstream schema gains id (slug kept as label). Repository threads the store id. Update store + contract-matrix tests. Migrate the 6 existing: assign ids 1-7 by creation order + git mv rename folders to NNNN-slug (handle the active workstream). DONE = contract matrix green for all backings; live .lume renamed sequentially; CLI behaviour-preserving; suite green. (Splittable if the diff is too large.)

E2: resolution + ordering. -w accepts an id OR a slug-label (unique scan; ambiguous/missing = named error); status queue + detail and other listings ordered by id; id surfaced in output (human + --json). DONE = -w by id and by slug both resolve; listings ordered by id; tests cover both; suite green.

E3: seed verb. lume seed [--new|--existing] creates the seed workstream (fs id 0 via seed=True + a seed attribute in the workstream record) and opens a discovery iteration with a mode-specific DoD skeleton (new: why/scope/constraints; existing: repo map); detection defaults by repo emptiness. DONE = lume seed creates id-0 seed + discovery iteration; new/existing modes covered; suite green.
E4: closeout - retro (did id-as-key + numbering + seed buy back their cost? contract evolution clean?), assess done-when clause by clause, lume close.
